_source: JALI (Edwards et al., SIGGRAPH 2016) https://www.dgp.toronto.edu/~elf/JALISIG16.pdf — rules and measured onset/decay constants, as plain data so new measurements drop in.

bilabial_close._note: hard constraint 1: bilabials close the lips (reproduces the legacy lips-closure floor)

labiodental_teeth._note: hard constraint 2: labiodentals draw the bottom lip to the teeth

sibilant_jaw._note: hard constraint 3: sibilants narrow the jaw

nonnasal_lip_open._note: hard constraint 4: non-nasal open segments open the lips (cap the closed-lip targets)

short_no_jaw._note: habit: a short (<max_dur s) obstruent or nasal leaves the jaw untouched — post-blend in apply_constraints, floor the jaw-class targets across the segment up to the lower of the two neighbouring (vowel) jaw levels, so a quick stop/nasal cannot dip the slow-moving jaw (it rides through on the surrounding vowels). This is a floor over the dense matrix, NOT a pre-blend weight mask like tongue_no_lip; it fires wherever the mapping exposes jaw-class targets, including the built-in viseme set (aa/E/I/O/U are jaw-class), so it does shape default JALI-on output (JALI-off is unaffected).

wordfinal_lip._note: habit: a word-final lip-shaped (lip_heavy) phoneme anticipates — extend its onset lead-in so the word-final lip shape forms early. Needs jali_timing, like lip_heavy. Word-final is approximated as pre-silence / utterance-final, because the phoneme stream carries no inter-word boundaries.

timing._note: measured onset/decay extents in seconds; onset ~120ms before the apex, ~120ms decay, tighter after a vowel, lip protrusion extends ~150ms
